label: Incident Response Consultant
description: Leads and manages cyber incident response engagements — detection, containment, eradication, recovery, and breach investigation across enterprise environments.

paths.name: Enterprise IR Track
paths.rationale: GCIH is the benchmark IR credential; GEIR adds advanced enterprise-scale response methodology; GCIL adds leadership depth for senior IR roles.
pros: GCIH is the most recognized IR credential and DoD approved
GEIR covers advanced enterprise IR methodology
Strong combination for consulting and enterprise IR team roles
cons: High cost without SANS training sponsorship

prerequisites.experience: SOC analyst or security operations experience strongly recommended.

cert_sequence.notes: GIAC Certified Incident Handler — the most recognized IR credential globally. Full incident handling lifecycle, attack techniques, network forensics in IR context, containment/eradication/recovery. Open-book — build indexed notes by phase. Required by name in most enterprise IR and MSSP job postings.
GIAC Enterprise Incident Response — advanced IR methodology covering enterprise-scale investigations, threat actor profiling during active incidents, and advanced containment strategies.

GIAC Cyber Incident Leadership — IR team management, stakeholder communication, regulatory notification requirements, and IR program governance. Essential for IR leads who interface with executives and legal counsel during major breaches.

paths.name: Practical IR Track
paths.rationale: Builds strong practical IR skill through hands-on certifications before the major GIAC investment. Good for practitioners building toward an IR consultant role from SOC foundations.
pros: BTL2 100% practical — proves genuine IR investigation skill
Good progression from SOC analyst toward IR consultant
cons: Lower employer name recognition than GCIH

prerequisites.experience: BTL1 or equivalent SOC experience before this path.

cert_sequence.notes: Security Blue Team Level 2 — covering threat hunting, malware analysis, and complex incident investigations. 24-hour practical exam. Demonstrates genuine IR investigation skill.
EC-Council Certified Incident Handler — broader IR methodology including legal aspects, regulatory considerations, and enterprise IR program design.
The senior recognition credential. With BTL2 and eCIH providing strong foundations, GCIH preparation focuses on deepening attack technique knowledge rather than learning IR concepts from scratch.

paths.name: OffSec IR Track
paths.rationale: OffSec's incident response cert combined with GCIH's recognized IR credential. OSIR provides practical hands-on IR investigation validation while GCIH covers the methodology and governance layer. Cheaper than the full GIAC enterprise IR stack while still including the most important GIAC credential.
pros: OSIR is 100% practical — validates genuine IR investigation skill
GCIH adds the most recognized IR credential alongside OffSec's practical proof
More affordable than GCIH + GEIR + GCIL stack
Strong combination of recognized methodology and practical validation
cons: OSIR has lower name recognition than GEIR at enterprise HR filters
OffSec IR certs newer — building market presence
Missing the leadership layer that GCIL provides in the full enterprise track

prerequisites.experience: SOC analyst or security operations experience recommended before this path.

cert_sequence.notes: GIAC Certified Incident Handler first — the most recognized IR credential provides the methodology foundation. Open-book exam, build indexed notes by incident phase. Getting GCIH early establishes the employer-recognized credential while you build toward practical depth.
OffSec Defense Analyst — adds practical defense analysis and malware behavioral analysis skills that complement GCIH's incident handling methodology. 24-hour practical exam.

OffSec Incident Responder — practical enterprise IR investigation. With GCIH providing the methodology framework and OSDA the detection analysis foundation, OSIR adds the hands-on investigation and containment skill validation. The combination of GCIH's recognized certification and OSIR's practical proof is a strong IR consultant credential stack.
